# SECTION 4 — Structural Diagrams & Schematics

## 4.1 Mermaid Compilation Safeguards Applied
- All node IDs are purely alphanumeric with letter prefix (e.g., `nodeA`, `step1`).
- No reserved keywords (`end`, `subgraph`, `graph`, `style`) used as node names.
- Node labels contain raw, clean uppercase alphanumeric text only — no markdown formatting or HTML inside the quoted labels.

---

## 4.2 Block-Level Architecture — DVR (Dynamic Voltage Restorer)

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    nodeA[SOURCE SUPPLY VS] --> nodeB[VOLTAGE SENSING UNIT PT]
    nodeB --> nodeC[CONTROL UNIT DSP / MICROCONTROLLER]
    nodeC -->|GATE PULSES| nodeD[VOLTAGE SOURCE INVERTER VSC]
    nodeD --> nodeE[LC FILTER]
    nodeE --> nodeF[SERIES INJECTION TRANSFORMER]
    nodeF -->|VINJ ADDED IN SERIES| nodeG[CRITICAL LOAD VL]
    nodeH[DC ENERGY STORAGE BATTERY OR SUPERCAP] --> nodeD
    nodeI[CT CURRENT SENSOR] --> nodeC
    nodeJ[BYPASS SWITCH] -.-> nodeA
    nodeJ -.-> nodeG
```

**Description of Flow:**
- The **Voltage Sensing Unit** monitors source and load voltages continuously.
- The **Control Unit** (DSP) computes the required injection voltage using **d-q transformation** and generates **PWM gate pulses** for the VSC.
- The **VSC** converts DC (from energy storage) to AC compensating voltage.
- The **LC filter** removes high-frequency switching ripple.
- The **series injection transformer** couples the voltage to the line at the appropriate level.
- The **CT** provides load-current feedback for closed-loop control.
- The **bypass switch** engages during DVR maintenance or catastrophic faults.

---

## 4.3 Block-Level Architecture — DSTATCOM

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    nodeA[LOAD AT PCC] --> nodeB[POINT OF COMMON COUPLING PCC]
    nodeB --> nodeC[SOURCE GRID]
    nodeD[VOLTAGE SENSOR V PCC] --> nodeE[SRF / P-Q THEORY CONTROLLER]
    nodeF[CURRENT SENSOR I LOAD] --> nodeE
    nodeE -->|GATE SIGNALS| nodeG[VSC IGBT INVERTER]
    nodeG --> nodeH[COUPLING INDUCTOR L F]
    nodeH --> nodeB
    nodeI[DC LINK CAPACITOR] --> nodeG
    nodeJ[VOLTAGE REGULATOR LOOP] --> nodeI
    nodeJ --> nodeE
```

**Description of Flow:**
- The controller uses **SRF theory** to extract fundamental positive-sequence voltage at PCC.
- Reference currents $i^{*}_{d}$ and $i^{*}_{q}$ are computed (where d-axis handles active power and q-axis handles reactive power).
- Hysteresis or PWM modulator drives the VSC.
- The VSC injects current through **coupling inductor L<sub>f</sub>** into the PCC.
- DC capacitor voltage is regulated to a fixed value (e.g., 700 V for 415 V system).

---

## 4.4 Block-Level Architecture — UPQC

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    nodeA[SOURCE] --> nodeB[SERIES VSC ACTS AS DVR]
    nodeB -->|INJECTS VINJ| nodeC[POINT OF COMMON COUPLING PCC]
    nodeC --> nodeD[SHUNT VSC ACTS AS DSTATCOM]
    nodeC --> nodeE[CRITICAL LOAD]
    nodeD -->|INJECTS ICOMP| nodeC
    nodeF[COMMON DC LINK CAPACITOR CD C] --> nodeB
    nodeF --> nodeD
    nodeG[UNIFIED CONTROLLER] --> nodeB
    nodeG --> nodeD
    nodeH[VOLTAGE AND CURRENT SENSORS] --> nodeG
```

**Description of Flow:**
- The **Series VSC** injects voltage to correct sags, swells, and source-side harmonics.
- The **Shunt VSC** injects current to correct load-side harmonics and reactive power.
- A **unified controller** (often a single DSP/FPGA) coordinates both converters.
- The **common DC bus** allows real power transfer between series and shunt converters — for example, during a voltage sag, the shunt converter draws extra real power from the source to recharge the DC bus, while the series converter injects the needed $V_{inj}$.

---

## 4.5 Block-Level Architecture — Single-Phase Active PFC Boost Converter

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    nodeA[AC MAINS 230 V 50 HZ] --> nodeB[DIODE BRIDGE RECTIFIER]
    nodeB -->|UNFILTERED DC| nodeC[BOOST INDUCTOR L]
    nodeC --> nodeD[SWITCH S MOSFET]
    nodeD --> nodeE[DIODE D]
    nodeE --> nodeF[OUTPUT CAPACITOR C OUT]
    nodeF --> nodeG[DC LOAD 400 V]
    nodeH[VOLTAGE FEEDBACK V OUT SENSE] --> nodeI[VOLTAGE ERROR AMPLIFIER]
    nodeI --> nodeJ[MULTIPLIER]
    nodeK[INPUT VOLTAGE SENSE FULL WAVE] --> nodeJ
    nodeJ -->|CURRENT REFERENCE I L STAR| nodeL[CURRENT ERROR AMPLIFIER]
    nodeM[INDUCTOR CURRENT SENSE I L] --> nodeL
    nodeL --> nodeN[PWM MODULATOR]
    nodeN -->|GATE DRIVE| nodeD
```

**Description of Flow:**
- The **input voltage sense** provides a rectified sinusoidal reference waveform to the multiplier.
- The **voltage error amplifier** regulates the output by slowly adjusting the amplitude of the current reference.
- The **multiplier** combines these to generate the **sinusoidal current reference** $i^{*}_{L}$.
- The **current error amplifier** forces actual inductor current to track the reference, producing a near-sinusoidal line current.
- The **PWM modulator** generates the MOSFET gate signal at fixed switching frequency (e.g., 100 kHz).
- The result: **input PF ≈ 0.99**, **THD<sub>i</sub> < 5%**.

---

## 4.6 Block-Level Architecture — Grounding and Wiring

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    nodeA[UTILITY SOURCE 11 KV] --> nodeB[DISTRIBUTION TRANSFORMER DY11]
    nodeB --> nodeC[MAIN PANEL BOARD]
    nodeC --> nodeD[GROUND BUS BAR]
    nodeD --> nodeE[EARTHING ELECTRODE ROD / PLATE]
    nodeC --> nodeF[SUB PANEL 1 POWER LOADS]
    nodeC --> nodeG[SUB PANEL 2 IT LOADS]
    nodeF --> nodeH[MOTORS RESISTIVE LOADS]
    nodeG --> nodeI[SERVERS NETWORK EQUIPMENT]
    nodeG --> nodeJ[ISOLATED GROUND RECEPTACLE]
    nodeD --> nodeJ
    nodeK[SINGLE POINT GROUND SPG] --> nodeJ
    nodeL[PROTECTIVE EARTH PE] --> nodeF
    nodeL --> nodeG
    nodeM[NEUTRAL N] --> nodeF
    nodeM --> nodeG
```

**Description of Flow:**
- The **distribution transformer** has its star point bonded to earth (forming the system ground).
- The **main panel** contains a single ground bus, which connects to the **earth electrode**.
- **Sub-panels** for IT/sensitive loads use **isolated ground receptacles** with a **single-point ground** to avoid ground loops.
- Power and IT sub-panels share the **PE conductor** but not the **signal-reference ground** (separation prevents noise coupling).
- The **MEN link (Multiple Earth Neutral)** — present in TN-C-S systems — combines neutral and earth at the main panel, then separates them downstream.

# SECTION 5 — KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

## 5.1 PART A — Short Answer Questions (3 Marks Each)

### Question 1 [KTU University Exam — June 2024]
**Q: Differentiate between voltage sag and voltage swell. State the role of a DVR in correcting them.**

**Model Answer (3 marks):**
- **Voltage Sag:** A reduction in RMS voltage to between **0.1 p.u. and 0.9 p.u.** for durations of **0.5 cycles to 1 minute**. Caused by motor starting, faults, transformer energization. (1 mark)
- **Voltage Swell:** An increase in RMS voltage to between **1.1 p.u. and 1.8 p.u.** for similar durations. Caused by sudden load removal, capacitor bank switching, single-line-to-ground faults. (1 mark)
- **Role of DVR:** A DVR is a **series-connected power electronic device** that detects sag/swell via voltage sensors and **injects a compensating voltage $V_{inj}$ in series** to restore the load voltage to nominal. During a sag, $V_{inj}$ is added in-phase; during a swell, $V_{inj}$ is injected with opposite polarity. (1 mark)

---

### Question 2 [KTU University Exam — December 2023]
**Q: List the main reasons for grounding an electrical system. Mention any two wiring problems that affect power quality.**

**Model Answer (3 marks):**
- **Reasons for grounding (any three, 1½ marks):**
  1. Personnel safety — limits touch voltage.
  2. Equipment protection from overvoltages and lightning.
  3. Voltage stabilization to a reference potential.
  4. Provision of fault-current path for overcurrent device operation.
  5. Reduction of EMI/RFI noise.
- **Two wiring problems affecting PQ (1½ marks):**
  1. **Shared neutrals** in polyphase circuits — cause harmonic neutral current (triplen harmonics, 3rd, 9th, 15th add in neutral).
  2. **Improper segregation** of power and signal cables — causes electromagnetic coupling and noise injection into data lines.
  3. **Loose/corroded connections** — cause voltage drops, arcing, and voltage sags at the load.

---

## 5.2 PART B — 14-Mark Questions (with Internal Choice)

### Question A (14 Marks) [KTU University Exam — June 2024 / Model Paper]

**Q: (a)** Explain the **block diagram, working principle, and operating modes** of a **Dynamic Voltage Restorer (DVR)**. With the help of a phasor diagram, derive the expression for the **injected voltage $V_{inj}$** when a three-phase balanced sag of depth $d$ occurs with a phase-angle jump of $\delta$. **(7 marks)**

**Q: (b)** A 3-phase, **415 V, 50 Hz** feeder feeds a sensitive load of **100 kVA** at 0.8 PF lagging. A balanced sag of **30% depth** with a **phase jump of $-5°$** occurs.
   (i) Calculate the **per-phase injected voltage** $V_{inj}$ required to fully restore the load voltage.
   (ii) Determine the **minimum DC link voltage** required for the DVR inverter.
   (iii) Calculate the **active and reactive power** handled by the DVR during the sag. **(7 marks)**

---

### Model Solution — Question A(a)

**Block Diagram:** (Draw using labels — Source → VSC + DC source → LC filter → Series injection transformer → Load) — **1 mark**

**Working Principle:**
1. Voltage sensors (PTs) measure $V_S$ and $V_L$ continuously.
2. Control unit (DSP) computes error = $V_{L,ref} - V_S$.
3. The required $V_{inj}$ is synthesized by a VSC using PWM.
4. $V_{inj}$ is filtered (LC) and injected via series transformer.
5. For a sag: $V_{inj}$ added in-phase; for a swell: $V_{inj}$ injected with 180° phase shift. **(2 marks)**

**Operating Modes:** (i) Standby / bypass, (ii) Sag injection, (iii) Swell injection, (iv) DC recharge — **1 mark**

**Phasor Diagram and Derivation:**

In a balanced 3-phase sag with depth $d$ and phase jump $\delta$:

$$
V_{S,sag} = V_{S,pre-sag}(1 - d)\angle\delta
$$

$$
V_{L,ref} = V_{S,pre-sag} \angle 0°
$$

$$
V_{inj} = V_{L,ref} - V_{S,sag}
$$

$$
V_{inj} = V_{S,pre-sag}\left[1 - (1-d)\angle\delta\right]
$$

Applying the cosine rule on the phasor triangle:

$$
\boxed{\vert V_{inj} \vert = V_{S,pre-sag}\sqrt{1 + (1-d)^{2} - 2(1-d)\cos\delta}}
$$

The phase angle of $V_{inj}$ is given by:

$$
\theta_{inj} = \tan^{-1}\!\left[\frac{(1-d)\sin\delta}{1 - (1-d)\cos\delta}\right]
$$

**(3 marks for derivation)**

---

### Model Solution — Question A(b)

**Given:**
- Line voltage = 415 V → $V_{S,ph,pre-sag} = \frac{415}{\sqrt{3}} = 239.6$ V
- $d = 0.30$, $\delta = -5°$
- $S_{load} = 100$ kVA at 0.8 PF lag → $P = 80$ kW, $Q = 60$ kVAr
- $I_L = \frac{100{,}000}{\sqrt{3}\times 415} = 139.1$ A
- $\theta_L = \cos^{-1}(0.8) = -36.87°$

**(i) Per-phase injected voltage magnitude:**

$$
V_{inj} = 239.6 \sqrt{1 + (0.7)^{2} - 2(0.7)\cos(-5°)}
$$

$$
= 239.6 \sqrt{1 + 0.49 - 1.4 \times 0.9962}
$$

$$
= 239.6 \sqrt{1.49 - 1.3947} = 239.6 \sqrt{0.0953} = 239.6 \times 0.3087
$$

$$
\boxed{V_{inj} = 73.96 \text{ V per phase}}
$$

**(2 marks)**

**(ii) Minimum DC link voltage (per phase):**

$$
V_{DC,min} = \frac{2 \sqrt{2} \cdot V_{inj}}{\sqrt{3} \cdot m_{a,max}}
$$

With $m_{a,max} = 1$ (linear modulation):

$$
V_{DC,min} = \frac{2\sqrt{2} \times 73.96}{1} = 209.2 \text{ V (phase), or  } \sqrt{3} \times 73.96 \times \sqrt{2} = 181.2 \text{ V (line-to-line in VSC output)}
$$

A practical design uses a safety factor of 1.5–2×, so $V_{DC} \approx 360$ V is selected. **(2 marks)**

**(iii) Active and reactive power handled by DVR:**

$$
P_{inj} = 3 \cdot V_{inj} \cdot I_L \cdot \cos(\theta_{inj} - \theta_L)
$$

Calculating $\theta_{inj}$:

$$
\theta_{inj} = \tan^{-1}\!\left[\frac{0.7 \times \sin(-5°)}{1 - 0.7 \cos(-5°)}\right] = \tan^{-1}\!\left[\frac{-0.0610}{0.3027}\right] = -11.4°
$$

$$
P_{inj} = 3 \times 73.96 \times 139.1 \times \cos(-11.4° - (-36.87°)) = 3 \times 73.96 \times 139.1 \times \cos(25.47°)
$$

$$
P_{inj} = 3 \times 73.96 \times 139.1 \times 0.9028 = 27{,}884 \text{ W} \approx 27.9 \text{ kW}
$$

$$
Q_{inj} = 3 \times 73.96 \times 139.1 \times \sin(25.47°) = 3 \times 73.96 \times 139.1 \times 0.4300
$$

$$
Q_{inj} = 13{,}283 \text{ VAr} \approx 13.3 \text{ kVAr}
$$

$$
\boxed{P_{inj} \approx 27.9 \text{ kW}, \quad Q_{inj} \approx 13.3 \text{ kVAr (per phase total)}}
$$

**(3 marks — 1 for P, 1 for Q, 1 for final boxed answer with units)**

---

### Question B (14 Marks) — Alternative Choice [KTU University Exam — December 2023]

**Q: (a)** Describe the **construction, working, and control** of a **DSTATCOM**. Compare it with a **UPQC** in terms of configuration and the type of disturbances each can mitigate. **(7 marks)**

**Q: (b)** Design a **single-phase boost-type Active PFC converter** for a SMPS rated **230 V, 50 Hz input; 400 V DC output, 1 kW**. The switching frequency is 100 kHz. Calculate:
   (i) The **boost inductor value** for CCM with ≤ 25% ripple.
   (ii) The **output capacitor** for a hold-up time of **20 ms** with $V_{out,min} = 300$ V.
   (iii) Sketch the **complete control block diagram** and explain the role of the **multiplier** and **voltage error amplifier**. **(7 marks)**

---

### Model Solution — Question B(a)

**DSTATCOM Construction (2 marks):**
- VSC (IGBT bridge) + DC link capacitor + coupling reactor + control system.
- Connected **in shunt (parallel)** at the PCC.

**DSTATCOM Working (2 marks):**
- Acts as a **synchronous voltage source** $V_{conv}$ behind reactance $X_L$.
- Reactive current $I_q = (V_{conv} - V_{PCC})/X_L$.
- If $V_{conv} > V_{PCC}$ → supplies reactive power (voltage boost).
- If $V_{conv} < V_{PCC}$ → absorbs reactive power (voltage reduce).
- Can also act as an **active harmonic filter** by injecting $180°$-shifted harmonic currents.

**Control of DSTATCOM (1 mark):**
- **SRF (Synchronous Reference Frame) theory** — Park's transformation isolates positive-sequence fundamental.
- **PI controllers** for DC-link voltage regulation and PCC voltage regulation.
- **Hysteresis-band current controller** or **SPWM/SVPWM** modulator drives the IGBTs.

**Comparison with UPQC (2 marks):**

| Feature | DSTATCOM | UPQC |
|---|---|---|
| Converters | One shunt VSC | Two VSCs (series + shunt) with common DC bus |
| Mitigates | Current harmonics, reactive power, voltage swell/sag (shunt-side) | **Both** voltage (sag, swell, imbalance, harmonics) and current (harmonics, PF) |
| DC source | Not needed (capacitor) | **Required** (battery or shared DC bus) |
| Cost | Lower | Higher |

---

### Model Solution — Question B(b)

**Given:** $V_{in,rms} = 230$ V, $V_{out} = 400$ V, $P_{out} = 1000$ W, $f_{sw} = 100$ kHz, $\Delta i_L = 25\%$ of peak current.

**(i) Boost Inductor (2 marks):**

$$
V_{in,peak} = 230\sqrt{2} = 325.3 \text{ V}
$$

$$
D = 1 - \frac{V_{in,peak}}{V_{out}} = 1 - \frac{325.3}{400} = 0.1868
$$

$$
I_{in,peak,avg} = \frac{2 P_{out}}{V_{in,peak}} = \frac{2000}{325.3} = 6.15 \text{ A}
$$

$$
\Delta i_L = 0.25 \times 6.15 = 1.537 \text{ A}
$$

$$
L = \frac{V_{in,peak} \cdot D}{\Delta i_L \cdot f_{sw}} = \frac{325.3 \times 0.1868}{1.537 \times 10^{5}} = \frac{60.77}{1.537 \times 10^{5}}
$$

$$
\boxed{L = 395 \text{ μH}}
$$

**(ii) Output Capacitor (2 marks):**

$$
C_{out} = \frac{2 P_{out} \cdot t_{hold}}{V_{out}^{2} - V_{out,min}^{2}}
$$

$$
= \frac{2 \times 1000 \times 0.020}{400^{2} - 300^{2}} = \frac{40}{160000 - 90000} = \frac{40}{70000}
$$

$$
\boxed{C_{out} = 571 \text{ μF (use 680 μF standard)}}
$$

**(iii) Control Block Diagram (3 marks):**

(Refer to the Mermaid diagram in Section 4.5. Sketch should include):
- **Input voltage sensor** → multiplier input.
- **Output voltage sensor** → **voltage error amplifier** (slow outer loop) → multiplier input.
- **Multiplier output** = $V_{in,rect} \times V_{ea,out}$ = current reference $i^{*}_{L}$.
- **Current sensor** → **current error amplifier** (fast inner loop).
- **PWM modulator** → MOSFET gate drive.

**Role of Multiplier:** Combines the rectified voltage waveform shape with the slow amplitude signal from the voltage error amplifier to create a **sinusoidal current reference** that has the correct shape (in phase with $V_{in}$) and the correct amplitude (to regulate $V_{out}$).

**Role of Voltage Error Amplifier:** Compares actual output voltage with reference and produces a **slow correction signal** that adjusts the amplitude of the current reference. This outer loop has a **low bandwidth (10–20 Hz)** to prevent it from distorting the 100 Hz rectified sine envelope.

## 5.3 KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning / Pitfall Callout

> [!WARNING]
> **Common Reasons for Mark Deduction in KTU 2024 Examinations:**
> 1. **Confusing DVR (series) with DSTATCOM (shunt):** Many students write "DSTATCOM injects voltage" — it injects **current**, not voltage.
> 2. **Omitting the phase jump $\delta$** in DVR calculations — results in under-rated DVR (loses 2–3 marks).
> 3. **Forgetting the $\sqrt{3}$ factor** in 3-phase calculations while using per-phase quantities — silent error.
> 4. **Writing PF = cos φ** in Active PFC — INCORRECT in the presence of harmonics. Must use $PF = \cos\phi / \sqrt{1 + THD_i^2}$.
> 5. **Confusing earth-resistance and touch-voltage limits:** $R_g \leq 5 \Omega$ is the **IS 3043** recommendation, but touch-voltage limit (50 V) depends on the disconnection time per **IEC 60479**.
> 6. **Drawing DVR/DSTATCOM block diagrams without labels for energy storage** — examiner expects a clear "DC source/battery" block.
> 7. **In Active PFC, failing to show the multiplier block** — without it, the design is incomplete.

---

## 5.4 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember

> **Rapid-Revision Checklist (Must Memorize for KTU 2024):**

- **DVR** = Series device, needs energy storage, corrects **voltage** problems.
- **DSTATCOM** = Shunt device, NO energy storage for VAR, corrects **current** problems + reactive.
- **UPQC** = Series + Shunt with **common DC bus** — the most powerful custom power device.
- DVR injected voltage formula: $\vert V_{inj} \vert = V_{S}\sqrt{1 + (1-d)^2 - 2(1-d)\cos\delta}$.
- DSTATCOM reactive current: $I_q = \Delta V_{PCC} / X_s$.
- Active PFC: Boost converter in CCM, $L = V_{in}D/(\Delta i_L f_{sw})$, $C_{out} = 2P_{out} t_{hold} / (V_{out}^2 - V_{min}^2)$.
- True PF includes both displacement AND distortion: $PF = \cos\phi / \sqrt{1 + THD_i^2}$.
- **Three top reasons for grounding:** Safety, Equipment Protection, Voltage Reference.
- **Touch voltage limit (IEC 60479):** 50 V AC dry, 25 V AC wet.
- **IEEE 519 limits:** Voltage THD ≤ 5% at PCC, Current THD varies with $I_{sc}/I_L$ ratio.
- **Grounding types (IEC 60364):** TN-S, TN-C, TN-C-S, TT, IT — know the MEN link concept.
- **Ground loops** = multiple parallel ground paths → noise; **single-point grounding** eliminates them.
- **Active PFC is mandatory** for SMPS > 75 W per IEC 61000-3-2.
- **Always specify modulation index $m_a$** in inverter sizing problems — default is $m_a = 1$ (linear).
- **DC link voltage requirement:** $V_{DC} \geq \sqrt{2} \times V_{LL}$ for SPWM with $m_a = 1$.
